name: Jewish "Corn" Bread (1 of 2)
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 cups All-purpose flour or as needed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine half the warm water, yeast, and sugar. Let stand until doubled in bulk (about 10 minutes).
2. Dissolve salt in remaining warm water.
3. Mix in sourdough starter, then yeast mixture, followed by gluten flour and all-purpose flour; form into a soft dough.
4. Knead on a floured surface to make a soft dough that holds its shape but is only slightly elastic and slightly sticky.
5. Form the dough into a ball and place in an ungreased bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise until doubled (about 1 1/2 hours).
6. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface, knead gently to release air, cover with a towel, and rest for 15 minutes.
7. Divide the dough into two portions. Shape each portion into an oval about 12 inches long, roll up from one side, pinch seam closed, and place on cornmeal-dusted baking sheets.
8. Cover with towels and let rise until nearly proofed (three-quarters proofed).
